entities.name: Mirage

attributes.title: nequissime
attributes.function: demonstrator of hidden knowledge, agent of will fulfillment

entities.raw_quote: Adiuro te, Mirage nequissime, inimicus fidei, humani generis mortis repertor, iniusticie declarator, malorum radix, fomes viciorum, seductor hominum, demonum magister...

entities.name: Satan
entities.raw_quote: Adiuro te, serpens antique, per iudicem viuorum et mortuorum...

entities.name: Ihesus Nazarenus

attributes.role: divine authority invoked to compel spirits
attributes.title: qui te creauit

entities.raw_quote: Imperat tibi Iesus Nazarenus, qui te creauit, vt cito impleas omnia que a te petam uel habere voluero uel scire desidero.

entities.name: Agnus inmaculatus

attributes.role: Christological title used as conjuratory authority

entities.raw_quote: Per agnum inmaculatum... qui ambulat super aspidem et basiliscum, qui conculcauit leonem et draconem [Ps 90:13]

entities.name: Alpha et O

attributes.role: divine name of God used in exorcism and binding

entities.raw_quote: per Alpha et O, per Ihesum Christum filium tuum...

attributes.role: ineffable divine name invoked for power
divine name invoked in binding and conjuration

entities.name: Sigillum Salomonis
entities.raw_quote: ...per omnes caractares, et per sigillum Salominis...

entities.name: Septem candelabra aurea

attributes.role: celestial symbols invoked in binding Mirage

entities.raw_quote: ...per annulos eius, per septem candelabra aurea ante dominum lucencia...

entities.name: Isaac
entities.raw_quote: ...Illum metue qui in Ysaac immolatus est...

attributes.role: invoked as source of binding power
Eucharistic body invoked for binding power

entities.name: Hostia sancta / inmaculata / conscripta / benedicta

attributes.role: various forms of Eucharistic offering invoked

entities.raw_quote: ...per hostiam sacram, per hostiam inmaculatam, per hostiam conscriptam et benedictam atque deo placentem...

summary: This chunk contains detailed conjuration formulas for binding and compelling spirits—specifically Satan and a spirit named Mirage—from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), as analyzed in Kieckhefer’s *Forbidden Rites*. The texts are exorcistic, Trinitarian, and draw heavily on biblical allusions, Eucharistic imagery, and Solomonine magical tradition. Multiple divine names (Alpha et O, Tetragramaton, Emanuel) and ritual tools (Solomon’s seal, seven golden candlesticks) are invoked to compel obedience without harm to the conjurer.
key_passages: Adiuro te, Mirage nequissime, inimicus fidei, humani generis mortis repertor, iniusticie declarator, malorum radix, fomes viciorum, seductor hominum, demonum magister...
Per agnum inmaculatum... qui ambulat super aspidem et basiliscum, qui conculcauit leonem et draconem [Ps 90:13 Vulg.], vt facias cito quidquid impero tibi et precipio.
per Alpha et O, per Emanuel, per nomen dei ineffabile, per sanctum Tetragramaton...

Exorcizo te, maledicte et mendacissime spiritus, • per verba veritatis, • per omnipotentem, • per Ihesum Christim Nazarenum, agnum immaculatum, de altissimo progressu[m], de Spiritu Sancto conceptum, ex Maria virgine natum...
Coniuro te et precipio tibi, N., • per hostiam sacram, • per hostiam inmaculatam, • per hostiam conscriptam et benedictam atque deo placentem.
